title: 10 best herbal teas for psoriasis

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Aloe vera tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin, aloe-emodin, and other polyphenols that can reduce inflammation and alleviate redness, swelling, and itching associated with psoriasis.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in aloe vera tea help neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells, promoting healthy skin cell turnover and reducing oxidative stress, which can contribute to psoriasis symptoms.

Moisturizing properties: Aloe vera tea is rich in vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals like calcium and potassium, which can help lock in moisture and soothe dry, itchy skin associated with psoriasis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, teas helps with psoriasis because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The soothing gel extracted from the aloe plant is known to calm irritated skin, reducing redness and discomfort associated with psoriasis. Drinking aloe vera tea may also help to reduce inflammation and promote skin cell growth, leading to improved skin health and potentially reducing psoriasis symptoms. Its natural compounds may also help to regulate the immune system, further alleviating psoriasis symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These glycosides exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with psoriasis.
Polysaccharides: Aloe vera's polysaccharides have been found to stimulate collagen synthesis and promote wound healing, which can aid in the repair of damaged skin in psoriasis patients.
Acemannan: A type of polysaccharide, acemannan has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, helping to regulate the immune response and reduce inflammation in psoriasis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The gel from Aloe vera leaves is commonly used to make teas for psoriasis due to its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves (Gel): The gel from the Aloe vera leaves is also commonly used to make teas for psoriasis because it contains vitamins and minerals that help to reduce inflammation and promote healing.
Leaves (Pulp): The pulp from the Aloe vera leaves is used to make teas for psoriasis as it is rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves from a mature plant with thick fleshy leaves.
Rinse the leaves under cold running water to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop the leaves into small pieces weighing approximately 20 grams per cup of tea.
Steep the aloe vera pieces in 1 liter of boiling water for 10 to 15 minutes.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve to remove the solids.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Stinging nettle tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like salicylic acid, which helps reduce inflammation and irritation associated with psoriasis, allowing for improved skin health.

Antioxidant properties: Nettle tea's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to psoriasis symptoms, promoting overall skin health and reducing oxidative stress.

Immunomodulatory effects: The immunomodulatory compounds in stinging nettle tea may help regulate the immune system's response to psoriasis triggers, reducing inflammation and promoting a more balanced immune response.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica, also known as stinging nettle, teas helps with psoriasis because it is rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and other beneficial compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help to reduce inflammation and soothe skin irritation associated with psoriasis. The tea also contains uric acid, which is involved in the regulation of skin health and can help to reduce the severity of psoriasis symptoms. Additionally, stinging nettle tea may also help to improve skin elasticity and reduce scaling, making it a potential natural remedy for managing psoriasis symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the inflammation and redness associated with psoriasis.

Phenolic acids (e.g., caffeic acid, ferulic acid): These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help protect the skin from damage and promote healing in psoriasis-affected areas.

Iridoid glycosides (e.g., aucubin, catalpol): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help regulate the immune system's response to psoriasis and reduce symptoms such as itching and scaling.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain compounds like salicylic acid and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and itching associated with psoriasis.
Roots: They are rich in antioxidants and other bioactive compounds that may help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing symptoms of psoriasis.
Stems: Like leaves, stems of Urtica dioica contain salicylic acid and other anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to treat psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the leaves and stems of the plant, cut them into small pieces, and dry them thoroughly in a cool place for 2 weeks.
Grind 2-4 teaspoons of dried plant material into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the ground plant material in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release its active compounds.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup, pressing on the solids to extract as much liquid as possible.
Allow the tea to cool to a comfortable drinking temperature, then consume it immediately to reap its potential benefits.

remedy_properties: Choleretic properties: Dandelion tea increases bile production, which helps to break down and remove toxins from the body, reducing inflammation and symptoms associated with psoriasis.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and taraxasterol present in dandelion tea have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching characteristic of psoriasis.
Antioxidant properties: Dandelion tea's high antioxidant content helps protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, reducing oxidative stress and promoting a healthy skin barrier.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion, teas helps with psoriasis because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The tea's compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote skin health. Dandelion tea may also help to balance the body's natural pH levels, which can become imbalanced in people with psoriasis. Additionally, the tea's diuretic properties can help to remove toxins from the body, reducing the severity of psoriasis symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: These compounds, particularly taraxasterol and taraxeryl acetate, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce the redness and inflammation associated with psoriasis.
Flavonoids: Quercetin, a flavonoid present in Taraxacum officinale, has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which may help alleviate psoriasis symptoms.
Saponins: These compounds have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antipruritic (itch-reducing) properties, which can help alleviate the itching and inflammation associated with psoriasis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part for making teas for psoriasis due to their high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds.
Roots: The roots of Taraxacum officinale are also used for teas, as they contain sesquiterpene lactones that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects.

Flowers: The flowers of Taraxacum officinale are used for teas due to their high content of flavonoids, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate psoriasis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest taraxacum officinale roots in late summer or early fall when they are at their highest concentration.
Dry the roots in a single layer at 40 degrees celsius for 24 hours to remove excess moisture.
Grind 2-3 grams of dried taraxacum officinale root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Steep 1 teaspoon of taraxacum officinale powder in 250 milliliters of boiling water for 5-10 minutes to release active compounds.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove solids and enjoy.

remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Marshmallow tea's high mucilage content helps to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with psoriasis.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in marshmallow tea help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to the development of psoriasis symptoms such as redness, itching, and scaling.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Marshmallow tea's anti-inflammatory compounds may help to reduce swelling and discomfort associated with psoriasis, making it easier for the skin to heal and reducing the risk of infection.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Althaea officinalis, also known as marshmallow, teas helps with psoriasis because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The mucilages present in the plant soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with psoriasis. The tea's demulcent action helps to lock in moisture, promoting a healthy skin barrier and relieving dryness and itchiness. Additionally, the antioxidant properties in Althaea officinalis may help to reduce oxidative stress, a known contributor to psoriasis symptoms. This natural remedy offers a holistic approach to managing psoriasis symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Mucilages help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and itching associated with psoriasis.
Gallic acid (a phenolic compound): Gallic acid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce the severity of psoriasis symptoms and prevent further skin damage.

Althaea officinalis polysaccharides: These polysaccharides have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help to reduce the inflammation and immune response associated with psoriasis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Althaea officinalis are primarily used to make teas for psoriasis due to their high mucilage content, which helps soothe and calm irritated skin.
Leaves: The leaves are used to make teas for psoriasis because they contain anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling associated with the condition.
Stems: The stems of Althaea officinalis are used to make teas for psoriasis due to their ability to provide relief from itchiness and irritation, often associated with psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of dried root or 4 ounces of fresh root of althaea officinalis in late summer or early fall.
Dry the harvested root in a single layer at 105 degrees fahrenheit for 2 to 3 hours.
Grind the dried root into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine 1 teaspoon of the ground root powder with 8 ounces of boiling water in a teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5 to 10 minutes then strain and serve while still warm.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tea contains calendula, which has anti-inflammatory compounds that can reduce swelling and redness associated with psoriasis.
Antioxidant: Calendula in pot marigold tea has antioxidant properties that can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate psoriasis symptoms.
Immunomodulatory: Pot marigold tea may modulate the immune system's response to reduce inflammation and prevent excessive cell growth, both of which are common issues in psoriasis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, teas helps with psoriasis because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The tea is rich in flavonoids and carotenoids, which help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the skin. Calendula's ability to soothe and calm irritated skin makes it an effective treatment for psoriasis symptoms such as redness, itching, and scaling. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Calendula officinalis teas can help to alleviate the discomfort and appearance of psoriasis.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant-derived compounds have potent anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and inflammation associated with psoriasis.
Carotenoids: Specifically, carotenoids like beta-carotene and lutein can help soothe and calm irritated skin, promoting healing and reducing the severity of psoriasis symptoms.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids in Calendula officinalis, such as caffeic acid and ferulic acid, possess strong antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in psoriasis-affected skin.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Calendula officinalis are commonly used to make teas for psoriasis due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used to make teas for psoriasis as they are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe skin irritations.

Roots: The roots of Calendula officinalis are sometimes used to make teas for psoriasis, but this is less common, as they are more typically used in tinctures and ointments for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 calendula officinalis flowers when they are in full bloom to ensure optimal potency.
Gently dry the flowers in a single layer on paper towels or a clean cloth for 7-10 days.
Store dried calendula flowers in airtight containers to preserve their medicinal properties for up to 6 months.
Use 1-2 teaspoons of dried flowers for every 8 ounces of boiling water and steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the solids to enjoy the warm, soothing liquid as needed.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and polyphenols, help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with psoriasis plaques, alleviating symptoms.

Antioxidant properties: The tea's antioxidant activity helps protect the skin from free radical damage, which can contribute to psoriasis development and exacerbate its symptoms by reducing oxidative stress.

Immunomodulatory properties: The tea's immunomodulatory compounds, such as terpenes and alkaloids, help regulate the immune system's response to psoriasis triggers, preventing excessive inflammation and cell proliferation that characterizes the disease.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, teas helps with psoriasis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The tea's flavonoids and terpenoids work to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which are major contributors to the development and exacerbation of psoriasis. By modulating the immune system and improving blood flow, Ginkgo biloba tea may help to reduce redness, itching, and scaling associated with the condition. remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce redness, itching, and swelling associated with psoriasis.
Bilobalide: This sesquiterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help soothe and calm the skin, reducing the severity of psoriasis symptoms.

Quercetin: As a flavonoid, quercetin has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which are often involved in the development and progression of psoriasis.

Seeds: They are a good source of ginkgolic acids, which have been traditionally used to treat skin conditions, including psoriasis, by reducing inflammation and promoting skin health.
Barks: They contain flavonoids and other compounds that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help soothe and calm psoriasis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried ginkgo biloba leaves per 8 ounces of water for a standard serving size.
Steep the dried leaves in boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes to release their active compounds.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the solids and leaves.
Add honey or lemon to taste if desired to enhance the flavor and palatability of the tea.
Allow the tea to cool to a comfortable temperature before serving and consuming it immediately.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Smooth Sumac tea's antioxidant compounds help neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress associated with Psoriasis.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rhus glabra, also known as smooth sumac, teas helps with psoriasis because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The tea has been traditionally used to reduce inflammation and itching associated with psoriasis. The plant's ability to reduce inflammation and promote skin regeneration may help in soothing the symptoms of the condition. remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and calm psoriasis symptoms.
Stems: Utilized for their potential to reduce inflammation and itching associated with psoriasis.
Barks: Employed for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help alleviate psoriasis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the rhus glabra leaves in early spring or late summer when they are at their highest potency levels.
Clean the harvested leaves thoroughly with water to remove any dirt or debris present.
Steep one teaspoon of dried rhus glabra leaves in one cup of boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine mesh sieve into a separate container to remove solids.
Store the herbal tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lavender tea's antioxidants help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can exacerbate psoriasis symptoms and promote skin cell growth.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lavender tea, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, may help reduce inflammation in the skin, which is a hallmark of psoriasis.

remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make teas due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated skin.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-60 grams of fresh or 20-40 grams of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer at 30-40 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Grind 2-3 teaspoons of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers into a fine powder using a mill.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the ground flowers in 250 milliliters of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and serve it immediately or store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator.

Tannins: Tannins in Sesamum indicum teas have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which can help to reduce redness, itching, and scaling associated with psoriasis.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 grams of dried sesamum indicum flowers at the peak of flowering stage.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the ground powder in 200 milliliters of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup.
Allow the tea to cool down completely before consumption within 10 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Lime tea's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to psoriasis symptoms like redness, itching, and scaling.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lime tea, such as flavonoids and limonoids, may help alleviate psoriasis symptoms by reducing swelling, redness, and pain associated with the condition.

Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and protect the skin from further damage.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Tilia europaea leaves are used to make teas for psoriasis due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Buds: Tilia europaea buds are used to make teas for psoriasis because they contain flavonoids that may help soothe and calm affected skin.
Leaves: Tilia europaea leaves are also used to make teas for psoriasis due to their antiseptic properties that can help reduce inflammation and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 tilia europaea leaves and flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Dry the collected leaves and flowers in a warm place for 2-3 days.
Chop 1 teaspoon of dried flowers and leaves into smaller pieces for infusion.
Steep the chopped mixture in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the infusion and let it cool to room temperature for 10-15 minutes before serving.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm teas helps with psoriasis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce redness and swelling associated with the condition. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves and stems from a trusted source.
Rinse the plant material with distilled water and gently pat dry with a cloth.
Measure out 20-30 grams of dried or 40-60 grams of fresh melissa officinalis per quart of boiling water.
Steep the plant material in boiling water for 5-7 minutes and then strain using a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before consuming or storing it in an airtight container.

Dry the freshly harvested leaves using a dehydrator or sun drying method for 24 hours to preserve flavor and nutrients.
Combine one teaspoon of dried symphytum officinale leaves with one cup of boiling water and steep for ten minutes to release active compounds.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids and discard any excess herbs or sediment.

Boil fresh water in a tea kettle for five minutes and let it cool slightly to avoid scalding.
Combine the harvested echinacea flowers with the cooled water in a large teapot or infuser.
Steep the mixture for seven to nine minutes allowing the herbs to release their medicinal properties.

Chamomile teas helps with psoriasis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe itchy and irritated skin. The apigenin in chamomile tea reduces inflammation, calms redness, and relieves discomfort associated with psoriatic plaques. Additionally, chamomile's antimicrobial agents help combat bacteria that can exacerbate psoriasis symptoms. remedy_recipe: Gather dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and boiling water for steeping.
Measure one teaspoon of dried herb per cup of boiling water in a tea infuser.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, allowing the herbs to release their active compounds.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth, discarding solids.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in fennel tea, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, help neutralize free radicals that contribute to skin cell damage and oxidative stress, common underlying factors of psoriasis.
Soften and moisturize skin properties: Fennel tea contains mucilages, which can help soften and soothe the skin, reducing itching, scaling, and inflammation associated with psoriasis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and itching associated with psoriasis, making it a popular choice for topical tea applications.
Bark: The bark of the fennel plant has been traditionally used to treat skin conditions like psoriasis due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one teaspoon of dried fennel seeds for every cup of boiling water you plan to use.
Heat the water in a teapot or kettle until it reaches a rolling boil before adding the fennel seeds.
Steep the fennel seeds in the hot water for 5-7 minutes to allow the flavors and beneficial compounds to meld.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth to remove the solids and retain the liquid.
Drink the tea immediately after preparation, allowing its anti-inflammatory properties to take effect within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric teas helps with psoriasis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce joint pain and swelling. Curcumin, a key compound in turmeric, inhibits the production of inflammatory chemicals that exacerbate psoriasis symptoms. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant properties help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and itching associated with the condition. By drinking herbal turmeric teas regularly, individuals with psoriasis may experience improved overall well-being and a reduction in psoriatic symptoms.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenol compound found in turmeric, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with psoriasis.

Cytokine modulation: Turmeric's bioactive compounds, including curcumin, can modulate the activity of cytokines, which are proteins involved in immune responses that contribute to psoriasis symptoms, thereby helping to regulate the immune system.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in turmeric tea, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can exacerbate psoriasis symptoms like redness, scaling, and itching.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the turmeric plant contain a high concentration of curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of psoriasis.
Rhizomes: Turmeric rhizomes are rich in antioxidants and have been traditionally used to treat skin conditions like psoriasis due to their ability to soothe and calm the skin.
remedy_recipe: Gather required ingredients including one teaspoon of dried turmeric root powder and eight ounces of boiling water.
Steep the turmeric powder in boiling water for five minutes to allow active compounds to infuse.
Strain the tea mixture into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids.
Serve the warm tea immediately after straining, ideally within one hour of preparation time.
Consume two cups of this turmeric tea per day for a minimum of four weeks to potentially alleviate psoriasis symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wintergreen teas helps with psoriasis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling associated with the condition. The tea's methyl salicylate content also soothes itching and discomfort, providing relief from the chronic skin condition. Additionally, wintergreen tea's antioxidant properties help to combat free radicals that can exacerbate psoriasis symptoms. By drinking this herbal tea regularly, individuals may experience a decrease in psoriasis symptoms, leading to improved overall health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Menthol content: Menthol in wintergreen tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to reduce redness and swelling associated with psoriasis.
Tannins: Tannins present in wintergreen tea act as astringents, reducing the severity of itching and inflammation by constricting blood vessels and reducing moisture levels on the skin.
Salicylic acid: Wintergreen tea contains salicylic acid, which is known to reduce inflammation and promote exfoliation, helping to remove dead skin cells and plaque associated with psoriasis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in salicylic acid, which helps reduce inflammation and relieve symptoms of psoriasis.
Rhizomes: Contain high levels of methyl salicylate, a natural anti-inflammatory that soothes skin irritations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems, ensuring they are free of contaminants and pesticides.
Combine 2 tablespoons of dried gaultheria procumbens leaves with 1 quart of boiling water, steeping for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding solids and reserving liquid.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the cooled tea, stirring until dissolved, and adjust dosage as needed.
Consume the tea once daily, ideally before bedtime, allowing for at least 30 minutes of digestion time.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena teas helps with psoriasis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herbal tea's flavonoids and terpenes work to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which are common underlying causes of psoriatic symptoms such as redness, itching, and scaling. By soothing and calming the skin, lemon verbena tea may help alleviate discomfort and promote a sense of relief, allowing individuals with psoriasis to experience improved overall well-being and quality of life.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Lemon verbena tea contains flavonoids, terpenes, and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with psoriasis.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in lemon verbena tea may help neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate skin inflammation and damage caused by psoriasis, promoting overall skin health and reducing symptoms.

Immunomodulatory effects: Lemon verbena has been shown to modulate the immune system's response, which could potentially reduce the inflammatory cascade triggered by an overactive immune response in psoriasis patients.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon verbena roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and itching associated with psoriasis.
Seeds: The seeds of the lemon verbena plant contain antioxidants and fatty acids that help soothe skin irritation and reduce redness.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried verbena officinalis leaves and stems and measure out two teaspoons for every eight ounces of water.
Bring the measured water to a boil in a teapot or saucepan then reduce heat to simmer for ten minutes.
Add the dried herb mixture to the hot water stir well then cover the pot and let steep for twelve to fifteen minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth discard the solids and add honey if desired.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking it within an hour of preparation for maximum potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort teas helps with psoriasis because its active compounds, such as hyperforin and hypericin, have potent anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce redness, swelling, and inflammation associated with psoriasis lesions. Additionally, St John's Wort has been shown to promote skin cell turnover, which can help to remove damaged skin cells that are characteristic of psoriasis. By reducing inflammation and promoting healthy skin cell growth, St John's Wort teas may help to alleviate symptoms of psoriasis such as itching, scaling, and redness.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This compound has potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with psoriasis.
Oligomeric flavonoids (OFs): OFs have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory molecules, thereby reducing inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to psoriasis symptoms.
Hypericin: This compound has antioxidant properties, which may help protect against DNA damage and reduce the risk of skin cancer associated with chronic psoriasis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of St John's Wort contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in reducing psoriasis symptoms.
Flowers: The flowers are rich in flavonoids and hyperforin, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and modulate the immune system, helping to alleviate psoriasis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hypericum perforatum leaves and flowers for best potency and aroma.
Combine 2 teaspoons of dried hypericum perforatum herb with boiling water to steep.
Allow mixture to simmer for 5-7 minutes to release optimal amounts of flavonoids and resin.
Strain the tea using a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove solids completely.
Drink the warm tea within 15-20 minutes to maximize bioavailability and absorption benefits.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to psoriasis that are treated with herbal teas are eczema, dermatitis, and acne. Herbs like calendula, chamomile, and green tea have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm irritated skin. Other herbs like turmeric, ginger, and neem have antimicrobial properties that can help reduce inflammation and prevent infection in the skin. Drinking these herbal teas can provide relief from symptoms such as itching, redness, and flakiness associated with these conditions.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for psoriasis are natural remedies that help alleviate the symptoms of this chronic skin condition. These teas are made from herbs rich in antioxidants, anti-inflammatory compounds, and vitamins that soothe and calm the skin. Examples include chamomile, turmeric, and ginkgo biloba teas, which reduce inflammation, promote skin healing, and improve overall well-being. Drinking these teas can lead to reduced skin irritation, improved skin elasticity, and enhanced quality of life for individuals suffering from psoriasis.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with psoriasis is a blend of calendula, chamomile, and green tea. Calendula tea, rich in anti-inflammatory properties, helps soothe and calm irritated skin. Chamomile tea, with its gentle antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, reduces inflammation and promotes relaxation. Green tea, high in antioxidants, protects the skin from damage and promotes healing. Drinking a warm tea infused with these herbs may help alleviate psoriasis symptoms and promote overall skin health. Consult a healthcare professional before trying any new remedies.
